name: Kaimuki Shokudo
tagline: Izakaya menu that actually runs late — Kaimuki residents' answer to where to eat at 11pm on a Saturday.
mustOrder: Garlic fried chicken — crispy, juicy, and perfect with a beer
Pork belly bao buns — rich and savory late-night fuel
Shoyu ramen — solid bowl to close out the night
parking: Street parking on Waialae Ave, side streets have more options
talkStory: Part of the Kaimuki dining renaissance on Waialae Ave, Shokudo brought a modern izakaya concept with late-night hours the neighborhood desperately needed. Open until midnight on weeknights and 1am on weekends — one of the few quality spots on Oahu serving food past 10pm. The garlic fried chicken and pork belly bao are designed for sharing over drinks. The late-night happy hour deals draw UH students and Kaimuki regulars. Sit at the bar for faster service and better people-watching.
